recording_notes: Phone filming RCA monitor — same skills lab as V11-V14 (Stryker, pegboard, FLS manual visible). Scissors resting in front of monitor. Pink/red foam block. White/light suture (new color). Suture debris on tape from many prior attempts.

frame_analyses.description: At 8 seconds, instruments in field near drain with suture loop visible. Light/white suture. Needle loading underway.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Fast entry. White suture is less visible than purple/blue suture used in V13/V14.

frame_analyses.description: At 40 seconds, still in suture placement. Both instruments near drain. Curved needle visible above drain being passed through. Suture driven through drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Extended placement — still placing at 40s. Slower than V12-V14 peak (22-25s).

frame_analyses.description: At 64 seconds, first throw in progress. Both instruments near drain. Suture crossing pattern visible. Slit beginning to close.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw underway. Placement completed around 48 seconds.

frame_analyses.description: At 96 seconds, third throw wrapping/tightening. Both instruments active near drain. Knot forming. Slit closing.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw in progress. Throw sequence proceeding at moderate pace.

frame_analyses.description: At 137 seconds, cutting phase. Right instrument across drain holding suture tail. Suture tail extending upward. Drain visible with knot and partially closed slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting in progress. Extended cutting phase.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame at 160 seconds. Instruments STILL ACTIVELY at drain — both visible crossing near knot area. Suture tail visible extending upward. Video ends with task completing at approximately 158 seconds.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completing right as video ends. Cutting phase at ~48 seconds is extended but improved from V16 (55s).

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain slit appears partially closed from monitor view. Small gap likely. No clean final frame with instruments withdrawn.

technique_summary: V17 at 158 seconds is a mid-range performance — faster than V16 (175s) but slower than V15 (142s). Suture placement at 36 seconds and cutting at 48 seconds continue to be the primary time sinks. The throw sequence at 62 seconds total is efficient. This video was recorded at the same skills lab as V11-V14, suggesting the trainee may be alternating between two training locations. The white/light suture is harder to track than colored suture.
improvement_suggestions: Cutting at 48 seconds still dominates — this is the single highest-ROI drill target
Suture placement at 36s is 14 seconds slower than peak — focus on efficient needle driving
Use colored (purple/blue) suture for better AI scoring visibility
If same session as V15/V16: this is attempt #3+ and fatigue is likely contributing
strengths: Throw sequence efficient at 62 seconds total
All three throws completed in correct sequence

key_observations: 158s / 435 FLS — mid-range, improved from V16 but below V12-V15
White suture less visible for AI scoring
17s faster than V16 suggests slight recovery but not back to peak
